steps.title: Use the given angle correspondence
steps.content: The similar-triangle statement gives the vertex correspondence directly: $R$ corresponds to $U$, and $S$ corresponds to $V$.

steps.title: In similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal
steps.content: When two triangles are similar, their corresponding angles have equal measures. We're told that triangles RST and UVW are similar with R corresponding to U and S corresponding to V. This correspondence tells us which angles must be equal.

steps.title: Use the correspondence to find angle V
steps.content: Since S corresponds to V, angle V must equal angle S. We're given that angle S = $58^{\circ}$, so angle V = $58^{\circ}$. B is correct.
